LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER'S MOTION TO FILE REDACTED EXHIBITS TO THE REPLY DECLARATION OF ANTHONY L. VALENZUELA, ESQ. IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED AND THE MOTION COMPLIES WITH CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT, RULES 1.201, 2.550 AND 2.551. THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE EXISTS AN OVERRIDING INTEREST THAT OVERCOMES THE RIGHT OF PUBLIC ACCESS TO THE PORTIONS OF THE RECORDS TO BE SEALED; THE OVERRIDING INTEREST SUPPORTS SEALING THOSE RECORDS; A SUBSTANTIAL PROBABILITY EXISTS THAT THE OVERRIDING INTEREST WILL BE PREJUDICED IF THE RECORD IS NOT SEALED; THE PROPOSED SEALING IS NARROWLY TAILORED; AND NO LESS RESTRICTIVE MEANS EXIST TO ACHIEVE THE OVERRIDING INTEREST. PETITIONER'S COUNSEL TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 2.550. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
Petitioner Charles Schwab Co., Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Redacted Exhibits To The Reply Declaration Of Anthony L. Valenzuela, Esq.
Off Calendar Jun-18-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mauricio B. Beugelmans, esq. (415-901-8752) of Schiff Hardin for Petitioner.
Michaele M. Gonzalez, esq. (619-299-4877) of Hamilton & Associates, APC for Respondent via Court Call.

NOT ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER CHARLES SCHWAB & CO INC.'S Motion To File Redacted Exhibits To The Reply Declaration Of Anthony L. Valenzuela, Esq.

Petitioner's motion to file redacted exhibits to the Reply Declaration of Anthony L. Valenzuela, Esq. is granted. No opposition filed and the motion complies with California Rules of Court, Rules 1.201, 2.550 and 2.551. The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

Petitioner's counsel to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 2.550.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition And Motion To Confirm Finra Dr Arbitration Award As To Respondent Linda T Chen
Off Calendar Jun-18-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mauricio B. Beugelmans, esq. (415-901-8752) of Schiff Hardin for Petitioner.
Michaele M. Gonzalez, esq. (619-299-4877) of Hamilton & Associates, APC for Respondent via Court Call.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER CHARLES SCHWAB & CO INC.'s Motion To Confirm FINRA-DR Arbitration Award As To Respondent Linda T. Chen:

Petitioner Charles Schwab & Co., Inc.'s petition and motion to confirm FINRA-DR arbitration award as to Respondent Linda T. Chen is granted. Contractual arbitration awards are subject to judicial review only on narrowly limited grounds. Respondent has not shown that any of the statutory grounds for vacating an arbitration award apply. (Code Civ. Proc. 1286.2.) The only ground Respondent raises is Code of Civil Procedure section 1286.2(a)(5), which states that the court shall vacate the award if "[t]he rights of the party were substantially prejudiced by the refusal of the arbitrators to postpone the hearing upon sufficient cause being shown therefor or by the refusal of the arbitrators to hear evidence material to the controversy or by other conduct of the arbitrators contrary to the provisions of this title." However, Respondent did not request the panel to postpone the hearing, and has not shown that the arbitrators' conduct violated any provision of the California Arbitration Act.

In any event, Respondent's assertion that she "was never served with the Statement of Claim prior to the hearing on the petition" is not supported by the record, which shows that on May 1, 2018, she was served with the Statement of Claim by FINRA at her home (and account) address, which constituted proper service under the parties' arbitration agreement. Respondent's assertion that she lacked capacity to defend against the arbitration is unsupported by a sufficient evidentiary showing. In any event, Respondent has not shown that she had any defense on the merits to Schwab's claim, and therefore cannot show that she suffered substantial prejudice. (See Swab Financial v. E*Trade Securities (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 1181, 1198 ["if there was an abuse of discretion, the trial court must determine whether the moving party suffered substantial prejudice as a result"]; Hall v. Superior Court (1993) 18 Cal.App.4th 427, 438-439 [trial court erred in vacating arbitration award where moving party failed to show substantial prejudice].)

Order and Judgment are signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
